goal.objective: Write a decision-grade comparison note about Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3-Codex, anchored in official pages and strengthened with selective independent evidence.
goal.context:
## Research strategy

1. **Start with the official pages** — scrape the two primary URLs below for first-party claims.
2. **Build normalized comparison axes** before drafting conclusions (same dimensions for both models).
3. **Run selective web research** for independent benchmarks, developer analyses, and expert commentary that validate, contradict, or add nuance to major claims.
4. **Scrape additional pages only when they materially improve decision quality** (not for redundant coverage).

Always attribute every claim to its source. If a claim appears only in the vendor's own page and no independent source corroborates it, mark it as *"vendor-stated, not independently verified"*.
If direct apples-to-apples comparison is impossible for a metric, state that explicitly instead of inferring.

Stop collection when:
- All must_have sections have enough cited evidence to draft confidently.
- Additional pages are mostly repeating already captured claims.
- New sources do not change the decision implications.

## Required final structure

1. Executive takeaway
2. Side-by-side comparison (normalized axes)
3. Independent validation and contradictions
4. Developer implications ("best fit when...")
5. Caveats, unknowns, and non-comparable metrics
6. Evidence quality classification

## Primary source URLs (scrape these first)

- https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-6
- https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-3-codex/

## Suggested web search queries (non-exhaustive, agents may add more)

- "Claude Opus 4.6 benchmarks 2026"
- "GPT-5.3-Codex benchmarks 2026"
- "Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3 comparison"
- "Claude Opus 4.6 developer review"
- "GPT-5.3-Codex developer review"
must_have: A concise side-by-side comparison of key claims using normalized axes (capabilities, benchmarks, long-context or agentic features, and product/API updates when stated).
Independent corroboration or contrast for major vendor claims, and explicit "not independently verified" labeling where corroboration is missing.
A practical "what this means for developers" section with "best fit when..." guidance and caveats about non-comparable metrics.
An evidence quality section that tags major conclusions as vendor-stated, independently-supported, or mixed.

Final markdown note (target 900-1300 words) at report/final-report.md. Every factual claim must be cited using short markdown links — e.g. ([Anthropic docs](url)) — never raw URLs. Collect all sources in a numbered "Sources" section at the end of the report.
HTML version of the final report at deliverables/report.html, rendered using the project template (render_html tool).
forbidden: Hype language, fanboy framing, or unsupported superiority claims
Presenting a single benchmark result without naming the source and methodology
Mixing up which claim comes from which source
Inferring superiority from non-comparable metrics without an explicit caveat

project.title: Decision-Grade Comparison: Claude Opus 4.6 vs GPT-5.3-Codex
project.description: A structured, evidence-based comparison of Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3-Codex, synthesizing official claims and independent validation, with actionable guidance for developers.

tasks.title: Collect and Structure Evidence with Source Traceability
tasks.body: Scrape the official pages for Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3-Codex. Build a structured evidence file capturing all key claims, benchmarks, features, and product/API updates, each with explicit source attribution. Run targeted web searches for independent benchmarks, developer reviews, and expert commentary. For each claim, indicate if it is vendor-stated, independently supported, or not independently verified. Organize evidence by normalized axes: capabilities, benchmarks, long-context/agentic features, product/API updates. Output a JSON file with traceable sources for each claim.
successCriteria: All key claims from both official pages are captured with source links.
At least two independent sources per model for major claims, or explicit 'not independently verified' tags where missing.
Evidence is organized by normalized axes and includes source traceability.
JSON file is complete and ready for synthesis.

tasks.title: Draft Side-by-Side Comparison Matrix
tasks.body: Using the structured evidence, create a markdown table or matrix that presents a side-by-side comparison of Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.3-Codex on the normalized axes: capabilities, benchmarks, long-context/agentic features, and product/API updates. Clearly mark which claims are vendor-stated, independently supported, or not independently verified. This matrix will be used as a core reference in the final report.
successCriteria: Matrix covers all normalized axes for both models.
Each claim is attributed and tagged for evidence quality.
No mixing of sources; all claims are clearly labeled.
Matrix is in markdown and ready for synthesis.

tasks.title: Synthesize Draft Report with Developer Guidance
tasks.body: Write a draft report (900-1300 words) following the required structure: executive takeaway, side-by-side comparison (using the matrix), independent validation/contradictions, developer implications ('best fit when...'), caveats/unknowns/non-comparable metrics, and evidence quality classification. Every factual claim must be cited using short markdown links. Include a numbered 'Sources' section. Mark all non-independently verified claims explicitly. The draft will be reviewed for citation integrity and adherence to must_have requirements.
successCriteria: Draft covers all required sections and word count.
All claims are cited with short markdown links and numbered sources.
Developer guidance and caveats are explicit and actionable.
No forbidden content is present.
Draft is ready for editorial review.

tasks.title: Editorial Quality Gate: Citation and Caveat Verification
tasks.body: Review the draft report for citation integrity, correct attribution, and explicit caveats for non-comparable metrics. Ensure all must_have requirements are met and forbidden content is absent. Refine language for clarity and neutrality. Approve or request fixes as needed. Output the final markdown report.
successCriteria: All citations are present, correct, and traceable.
All non-comparable metrics are explicitly caveated.
No forbidden content or unsupported claims.
Final markdown report is ready for HTML rendering.

tasks.title: Render Final Report to HTML
tasks.body: Render the final markdown report to HTML using the project template and the render_html tool. Ensure formatting, links, and section structure are preserved. Output the HTML file to the deliverables directory.
successCriteria: HTML version matches the markdown report in content and structure.
All links and formatting are correct.
HTML file is ready for delivery.

assumptions: Official pages and independent sources are accessible for scraping and search.
Word count and citation requirements are strictly enforced.
All agents have access to required tools and workspace paths.
risks: Independent corroboration may be limited for very recent model releases.
Some metrics may not be directly comparable; explicit caveats are required.
Strict citation and evidence quality tagging may increase editorial workload.
